headline: Minutes of the Board of Directors — Feb-Interim 2028 Regular Meeting
rendered_text:
# MINUTES OF THE BOARD OF DIRECTORS
## OF
## ECHOBLAST, INC.
### a Delaware corporation

**Regular Meeting**
**February 15, 2028, 9:00 a.m. Pacific Time**
**500 Sansome Street, San Francisco, CA 94111**

---

## CALL TO ORDER AND QUORUM

The Regular Meeting of the Board of Directors of Echoblast, Inc. (the "Company") was called to order by Will Coleman, Chief Executive Officer and Chairman of the Board, at 9:03 a.m. Pacific Time on February 15, 2028.

Miguel Black, General Counsel and Secretary, noted that a quorum was present, constituting a majority of the Board's authorized directors. The following directors were present in person:

- Will Coleman, CEO and Chairman
- Steven Morris, Director (Greylock Partners)
- Mark Howell, Chief Technology Officer and Co-Founder
- Angela Holmes, President and Co-Founder

The following non-voting observer was also present:

- Akira Wright, Board Observer (Conviction Partners)

Mark Leonard, Chief Architect and Co-Founder, was absent from the meeting due to a prior commitment.

Mr. Black confirmed that notice of the meeting had been duly given to all directors in accordance with the Company's bylaws and Delaware law.

---

## APPROVAL OF PRIOR MEETING MINUTES

Mr. Coleman presented the minutes of the Regular Meeting of the Board held on January 18, 2028, which had been circulated to all directors prior to this meeting.

**RESOLVED**, that the minutes of the Regular Meeting of the Board held on January 18, 2028, are hereby approved as circulated.

**Vote:** Approved unanimously.

---

## CONFLICTS OF INTEREST AND RECUSALS

Mr. Black inquired whether any director had a material conflict of interest with respect to any matter on the agenda for this meeting. No conflicts were disclosed. All directors confirmed their ability to participate fully in all matters to be discussed.

---

## CEO REPORT

Mr. Coleman delivered a comprehensive strategic update covering operational highlights, market positioning, and near-term priorities.

**Operational Highlights and Customer Pipeline**

Mr. Coleman reported that the Company continues to demonstrate strong momentum in the GPU rental and managed AI workload markets. The Company has expanded its customer base across multiple verticals, including generative AI model development, enterprise machine learning, and academic research institutions. Several large enterprise prospects remain in advanced stages of negotiation, with anticipated contract closures in Q1 2028.

The Company's platform adoption metrics remain healthy, with increasing utilization rates across existing customer cohorts. Customer satisfaction scores (NPS) remain above 65, reflecting strong product-market fit and operational execution.

**Product Roadmap and Technical Milestones**

Mr. Howell, Chief Technology Officer, provided an update on the product roadmap. The engineering team has successfully deployed enhanced managed inference capabilities, enabling customers to run production workloads with improved latency guarantees and cost predictability. A new feature enabling multi-region failover and workload migration is scheduled for general availability in March 2028.

The Company's internal on-call rotation for fleet operations continues to leverage PagerDuty with a 15-minute acknowledgment SLA, ensuring rapid response to any platform incidents. Uptime metrics for Q1 2028 (through February 15) remain above 99.95%.

**Marketplace Dynamics and Third-Party Capacity**

Mr. Coleman noted that the Company's marketplace for third-party GPU capacity continues to grow as a material revenue stream. The Company maintains an 18% marketplace take rate on all end-customer spend routed through third-party capacity bookings. This channel is expected to contribute meaningfully to revenue growth in 2028, as the Company expands partnerships with independent compute providers and regional data center operators.

**Competitive Landscape**

Mr. Coleman observed that competitive intensity in the GPU rental and managed AI infrastructure space remains elevated, with several well-capitalized entrants continuing to invest heavily in marketing and customer acquisition. However, the Company's differentiation—centered on ease of use, superior customer support, and transparent pricing—continues to resonate with customers. The Company does not anticipate material pricing pressure in the near term, though the Board was advised to monitor competitive developments closely.

**Organizational Updates**

The Company currently maintains a headcount of 64 employees, distributed across engineering, sales, customer success, operations, and finance functions. Hiring remains focused on senior engineering roles and customer success specialists to support anticipated customer growth. The Company expects to reach approximately 75 headcount by end of Q2 2028.

---

## CFO REPORT

Ms. Angela Holmes, President and interim Chief Financial Officer, presented a comprehensive financial review for the period ended January 31, 2028.

**Revenue and ARR Performance**

Ms. Holmes reported that Annual Recurring Revenue (ARR) reached $42.1 million as of January 31, 2028, representing sequential growth of 8.2% from the prior month. Total revenue for January 2028 was $3.511 million, consistent with the Company's growth trajectory and in line with internal forecasts.

The Company's revenue is derived from three primary sources: (1) managed GPU rental services (approximately 62% of revenue); (2) managed training and inference services (approximately 28% of revenue); and (3) marketplace take rates on third-party capacity bookings (approximately 10% of revenue). All three segments demonstrated month-over-month growth.

**Gross Margin and Operating Metrics**

Ms. Holmes reported that gross margin in January 2028 was 68%, reflecting operational efficiency gains and improved utilization of the Company's owned GPU fleet. Cost of revenue remains primarily driven by GPU acquisition costs and cloud infrastructure expenses, which the Company continues to optimize through long-term capacity agreements with major GPU suppliers.

Operating expenses (excluding cost of revenue) for January 2028 totaled $2.84 million, consisting of sales and marketing ($1.12M), research and development ($1.35M), and general and administrative expenses ($0.37M). The Company's operating loss for January was $0.89 million, reflecting continued investment in product development and sales capacity ahead of anticipated revenue acceleration.

**Cash Position and Runway**

Ms. Holmes reported that the Company's cash position as of February 1, 2028 was $18.7 million, consisting of $14.2 million in operating cash and $4.5 million in restricted cash (held against customer deposits and platform escrow accounts). Based on current burn rate of approximately $0.85 million per month, the Company has approximately 22 months of cash runway, excluding anticipated revenue growth and any future capital raise.

The Board noted that the Company is not currently fundraising and does not anticipate the need for additional capital in 2028, barring a material change in strategic direction or market conditions.

**Capital Allocation and Budget Variance**

Ms. Holmes reported that the Company's year-to-date performance (January 2028) is tracking to plan against the 2028 operating budget approved by the Board in December 2027. No material variances were identified. The Company remains disciplined in its approach to capital allocation, prioritizing investments in product development and customer acquisition over non-essential discretionary spending.

---

## COMMITTEE REPORTS

The Board noted that formal committee meetings are anticipated to be established following the Company's anticipated initial public offering in Q1 2030. Until such time, the Board continues to operate on a full-board basis with periodic deep dives on audit, compensation, and governance matters.

Mr. Coleman noted that the Board will establish an Audit Committee, Compensation Committee, and Nominating and Governance Committee in connection with IPO preparation, anticipated to commence in late 2029.

---

## STRATEGIC DISCUSSION ITEMS

**Data Center and Infrastructure Expansion**

Mr. Coleman and Mr. Howell discussed the Company's strategy for geographic expansion of GPU capacity. The Company currently operates managed capacity in three U.S. regions (Northern California, Virginia, and Oregon) and has initiated exploratory discussions with data center operators in Europe and Asia-Pacific to support anticipated international customer demand.

Capital expenditure requirements for infrastructure expansion are anticipated to be approximately $4.2 million in 2028, allocated to GPU acquisition and deployment across existing and new regions. The Board approved this allocation as consistent with the Company's growth strategy and cash position.

**Internal Collaboration Infrastructure**

Mr. Howell provided an update on the Company's internal technology stack, which continues to leverage Slack for team communication, Linear for engineering project management, Notion for documentation and knowledge management, and GitHub Enterprise for source code management and CI/CD workflows. These tools have enabled the Company to maintain operational efficiency and knowledge sharing across a growing distributed engineering team.

**Customer Concentration Risk**

Ms. Holmes presented an analysis of customer concentration risk. The Company's top 10 customers represent approximately 34% of total ARR, with the largest single customer representing 6.2% of ARR. The Board discussed the importance of continued customer diversification and noted that the sales team is actively pursuing opportunities across multiple customer segments to reduce concentration risk over time.

**Regulatory and Compliance Updates**

Mr. Black, General Counsel, provided a brief update on regulatory and compliance matters. The Company continues to monitor developments in AI regulation and data privacy law at the federal and state levels. The Company has implemented SOC 2 Type II controls and is in process of obtaining ISO 27001 certification, both of which are anticipated to be completed by end of Q2 2028. These certifications are expected to facilitate sales to enterprise customers with stringent security and compliance requirements.

---

## RESOLUTIONS AND VOTES

**Authorization of 2028 Capital Expenditure Plan**

**RESOLVED**, that the Board of Directors hereby approves the 2028 Capital Expenditure Plan, allocating approximately $4.2 million to GPU acquisition, deployment, and data center infrastructure expansion across the Company's operating regions, as presented by the Chief Executive Officer and President.

**Vote:** Approved unanimously.

---

## EXECUTIVE SESSION

At 10:47 a.m., Mr. Coleman noted that the Board would enter executive session to discuss confidential matters relating to management performance and strategic initiatives. Mr. Howell, Ms. Holmes, and Mr. Leonard (by proxy) departed the meeting. Mr. Black, as General Counsel, remained present to provide legal counsel.

The Board, consisting of Mr. Coleman and Mr. Morris, convened in executive session. Mr. Wright, as a non-voting observer, also departed.

**Executive Session Discussion**

The Board discussed confidential operational and strategic matters, including management performance and compensation considerations. Details of this discussion are maintained in a separate privileged memorandum maintained by the General Counsel.

The executive session concluded at 11:12 a.m., and management rejoined the meeting.

---

## NEXT MEETING DATE

Mr. Coleman noted that the next Regular Meeting of the Board is scheduled for March 20, 2028, at 9:00 a.m. Pacific Time, at the Company's principal offices in San Francisco. The Board Secretary will circulate an agenda and supporting materials at least five business days prior to the meeting.

---

## ADJOURNMENT

There being no further business to come before the Board, the meeting was adjourned at 11:18 a.m. Pacific Time.

**RESOLVED**, that the meeting is hereby adjourned.

---

## SECRETARY ATTESTATION

Respectfully submitted,

/s/ Miguel Black

**Miguel Black**
General Counsel and Secretary of the Meeting

**Date:** February 15, 2028

---

*These minutes constitute the official record of the Regular Meeting of the Board of Directors of Echoblast, Inc. held on February 15, 2028. This document is confidential and intended solely for the use of the Board of Directors and authorized officers of the Company.*
